data.title: AI Adoption May Lead to Industry Consolidation, Says ServiceNow COO
data.description: The potential for consolidation in the software industry looms as companies struggle to adapt to artificial intelligence, according to ServiceNow COO Amit Zavery. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Zavery's insights on the challenges faced by firms in integrating AI into their operations. As AI continues to reshape the technological landscape, businesses that fail to evolve may find themselves at a disadvantage, potentially leading to mergers or acquisitions. Zavery's comments underscore the importance of innovation and adaptability in maintaining a competitive edge in the rapidly changing software sector.

data.title: Civil Rights Leader Jesse Jackson Passes Away at 84
data.description: Civil rights leader Jesse Jackson has died at the age of 84. According to RTHK, Jackson was a prominent figure in the 1960s civil rights movement, working alongside the late Martin Luther King Jr. to advocate for the rights of African Americans in the United States. Following King's legacy, Jackson emerged as a leading figure in the civil rights movement, gradually extending his influence into the political arena. He ran twice for the Democratic presidential nomination, although he did not succeed. His efforts are credited with paving the way for former President Barack Obama and former Vice President Kamala Harris to enter the core of White House power.

data.title: Coinbase’s Base Moves Beyond OP Stack to Boost Decentralization
data.description:
Coinbase’s Layer-2 network Base has announced a major architectural shift, signaling a move away from exclusive reliance on the OP Stack as it rebuilds core infrastructure for a more decentralized future.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/coinbases-base-moves-beyond-op-stack-to-boost-decentralization/

data.title: AI Disruption Could Cut Creator Earnings by Nearly 25% by 2028, UNESCO Warns
data.description:
A new UNESCO report projects steep revenue losses for music and screen creators as lawyers say the fair use doctrine is buckling under AI’s scale.
source: https://decrypt.co/358535/ai-disruption-creator-earnings-unesco

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Significant Net Outflows
data.description:
According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that on February 18 (Eastern Time), Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $133 million.

The largest single-day net outflow was recorded by Blackrock's ETF IBIT, with $84.19 million leaving the fund. Despite this, IBIT's historical total net inflow stands at $61.403 billion.

Following IBIT, Fidelity's ETF FBTC experienced a net outflow of $49.0749 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $10.939 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $83.625 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 6.31% compared to Bitcoin's total market value. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $54.091 billion.

data.title: India's Brokerage Lobby Seeks Delay in Central Bank's New Lending Rules
data.description: India's brokerage lobby group has reached out to the market regulator, requesting intervention regarding the central bank's new regulations on bank lending to traders. According to Jin10, the lobby group is advocating for a six-month postponement of the implementation of these recent rules introduced by the Reserve Bank of India. The request highlights concerns within the financial sector about the immediate impact of the new lending guidelines on market operations and liquidity. The central bank's regulations aim to tighten control over bank lending practices, but the brokerage community argues that more time is needed to adjust to these changes. The outcome of this request could significantly influence the trading environment and financial market stability in India.

data.title: Tech Entrepreneur Mark Shuttleworth to Fund Biodiversity Efforts on Principe
data.description: Tech entrepreneur Mark Shuttleworth is set to begin distributing a 'natural dividend' to the residents of Principe this month to support biodiversity conservation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Shuttleworth's initiative aimed at preserving the island's unique ecosystem. This effort underscores the growing trend of private individuals investing in environmental sustainability projects. Shuttleworth's commitment reflects a broader movement towards integrating ecological preservation with community development, providing financial incentives to local populations to maintain and protect their natural surroundings. The initiative is expected to have a significant impact on both the environment and the local economy, fostering a sustainable future for Principe.

data.title: South Korea's Auto Exports Surge in January Driven by Eco-Friendly Vehicles
data.description: South Korea's government announced on Thursday that the country's automobile exports increased by 21.7% in January compared to the same period last year. According to Jin10, this growth is attributed to the substantial export of eco-friendly vehicles and an increase in the number of working days. Data from the Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Energy revealed that last month's total automobile export value reached $6.07 billion, marking the second-highest record for the same period. This surge was primarily driven by strong demand for electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrid cars. EV exports rose by 21.2% year-on-year, reaching $780 million, while hybrid car exports soared by 85.5%, totaling $1.71 billion. By destination, exports to the United States increased by 19.2% to $2.66 billion, and exports to the European Union grew by 34.4% to $871 million. However, exports to Asia decreased by 30.1%, falling to $325 million.

data.title: New Zealand Reserve Bank Maintains Interest Rates Amid Economic Recovery Expectations
data.description: The New Zealand Reserve Bank has decided to keep interest rates steady at 2.25%, anticipating a robust economic recovery without triggering inflationary pressures. According to Jin10, Assistant Governor Silk highlighted the unique position of the central bank this year, expecting economic growth to accelerate to 2.8% by March 2027 while inflation slows to the target level of 2%. Silk explained the core discussion of the recent meeting centered on the belief that economic growth and inflation reduction can occur simultaneously. Despite the apparent contradiction, Silk noted that the output gap allows for strong growth, potentially surpassing the potential growth level during this period. Investors have fully priced in a 25 basis point rate hike by December.

data.title: Airbus Faces Production Delays Due to Engine Supply Shortages
data.description: Airbus has announced that the production and delivery of its A320 family of jets are being hindered by unreliable engine supplies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the aerospace giant in maintaining its manufacturing schedule. The company is experiencing difficulties in securing consistent engine deliveries, which is impacting its ability to meet demand for the popular aircraft series. Airbus is working to address these supply chain issues to ensure timely delivery of its jets.

data.title: Russian Forces Encounter Communication Challenges Amid Starlink and Telegram Restrictions
data.description: Russian troops engaged in the conflict in Ukraine are experiencing significant communication difficulties. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the dual impact of restricted access to Elon Musk's Starlink mobile internet system and the Kremlin's tightening control over the Telegram messenger service. These issues are complicating efforts for Russian forces, affecting their operational capabilities in the region. The limitations on Starlink and Telegram are part of broader measures impacting communication channels, posing challenges for military coordination and information exchange.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $133 million yesterday, with none of the twelve ETFs experiencing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $133 million yesterday (February 18th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net outflow of $84.1949 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $61.403 billion. This was followed by Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a single-day net outflow of $49.0749 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $10.939 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $83.625 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.31%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $54.091 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $41.8274 million yesterday, with none of the nine ETFs experiencing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $41.8274 million yesterday (February 18th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $29.9343 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.97 billion. This was followed by Fidelity ETF FETH, with a single-day net outflow of $8.2252 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.479 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.121 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.75%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.655 billion.

data.title: U.S. Congress to Discuss CLARITY Act's Impact on Stablecoin Revenue
data.description: The U.S. Congress is preparing for its third meeting concerning the CLARITY Act, which addresses the structure of the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, this session is scheduled for February 20 at 2:00 p.m. UTC and will focus on the revenue generated by stablecoins. The meeting will feature key participants from both the cryptocurrency and banking sectors.

data.title: Landslide Strikes Indonesia's Major Nickel Smelting Complex
data.description: A landslide has occurred at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park, one of the largest nickel smelting complexes globally. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this incident is the latest in a series of accidents affecting the facility. The industrial park, known for its significant contribution to nickel production, has faced multiple challenges recently, raising concerns about safety and operational continuity. The impact of the landslide on production and the broader nickel market remains to be seen, as authorities assess the situation and work towards mitigating further risks.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank to Increase Policy Decision Frequency Starting 2027
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand has announced a change in its policy decision schedule, increasing the number of annual meetings from seven to eight starting in 2027. According to Jin10, this adjustment is in response to the shift in the release of Consumer Price Index (CPI) data from quarterly to monthly beginning next year. To accommodate the new schedule, the February 2027 decision date will be moved forward by one week.

Although the central bank has set its decision dates through February 2028, the Monetary Policy Committee retains the ability to make unscheduled decisions if financial or economic conditions warrant. Analysts have noted that this change is procedural rather than directional. However, the increased frequency of meetings may slightly reduce policy lag effects and could heighten the sensitivity of the New Zealand dollar and interest rates to unexpected changes in monthly CPI data.

data.title: Denmark Faces Economic Challenges Amid Corporate Layoffs
data.description: Denmark's economy appears robust, yet recent mass layoffs at major companies like Novo Nordisk have raised concerns about potential further job cuts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these layoffs on the Danish workforce. Despite positive economic indicators, the reality of job losses is prompting a reevaluation of the country's economic stability. As companies reassess their workforce needs, the threat of additional layoffs looms, challenging the perception of Denmark's economic strength.

data.title: US CLARITY Act to pass 'hopefully by April': Senator Bernie Moreno
data.description:
Odds of the US CLARITY Act passing in 2026 briefly spike to 90% on Polymarket on Wednesday amid optimistic comments from US Senator Bernie Moreno.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-us-clarity-act-coinbase-brian-armstrong-bernie-moreno?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
